affected_groups: Families and pupils currently on or planning a selective-track (grammar school) path
State-sector general schools that must absorb displaced pupils
School maintainers (state, church, foundation) affected asymmetrically by capacity policy
content.decision_question: Predictability and procedural fairness versus administrative flexibility in managing shrinking capacity
content.evidence_boundary: Contested: whether current or proposed capacity-management mechanisms in scenario S2 actually lack a published rationale/schedule, and whether the burden falls disproportionately on state-sector families relative to church/foundation providers, is not settled in the record and depends on implementation details not yet specified
content.tension: Arbitrary, discretionary capacity cuts disrupt families who already committed to a selective-track path and provide no predictable, published rationale or schedule for planning.
content.title: Decision tension A6: S2
value_poles: administrative flexibility in managing shrinking capacity
